audit_method: direct URL fetch — strip-first repair, 2026-06-12. Each source URL fetched directly with curl or PDF text extraction; no WebSearch snippets used.

rows.supporting_quote: In 1982, the first Intergovernmental Agreement (IGA) between the City and County was signed creating the Aspen/Pitkin County Housing Authority (APCHA) as an independent multi-jurisdictional housing authority charged with overseeing affordable housing for persons of low, moderate, and middle incomes, who are also permanent residents and employed full-time in the City and County. APCHA's powers are multi-jurisdictional, and allow the program to acquire and dispose of property; plan, construct, and manage affordable housing; make contracts; hire employees; and raise revenues to fund the program.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Original demolition-history clause was dropped because the source did not support it.

rows.supporting_quote: Rental Units are available in Categories 1 through 5 and in the RO Category, and Ownership Units are available in Categories 1 through 5 and in the RO Category as provided in the applicable deed restriction. The homes will continue to appreciate as permitted under their respective deed restrictions (in most cases, 3% or the consumer price index, whichever is less, per year).
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext. Original s2 inventory claim was dropped because the Aspen Daily News page returned HTTP 429 and could not support the total inventory figures.

rows.supporting_quote: Seventy percent of the full-time occupied units in Aspen are deed-restricted – the second-highest rate among 38 rural and resort communities in Colorado and five out-of-state ski towns inventoried in a recent survey. ... Among the 3,278 full-time occupied units in Aspen, 70% are deed-restricted as of July, for a total of 2,303.

City of Aspen voters approved a 1.0% real estate transfer tax for the purpose of "addressing the issue of the current housing shortage" and became effective July 1, 1989. On May 8, 1978, voters were asked and supported a 0.5% real estate transfer tax for the "purpose of renovation, reconstruction and maintenance of the Wheeler Opera House and for the purpose of supporting the visual and performing arts." ... Transfer taxes are the responsibility of the purchasing party.

Issue 2A passed with 66.00% voting "yes" (2,628 votes) to 34.00% voting "no" (1,345 votes). This measure proposes extending the existing 1% real estate transfer tax and 0.45% sales tax, which fund affordable housing and transportation initiatives in Aspen.

The city of Aspen collected nearly $24 million in taxes paid at the closing of 638 free-market real estate deals in 2024. ... RETT collections of $23.9 million beat the revenue source's 2024 budgeted figure by $4.5 million. ... Of the $23.9 million, $15.8 million will fund housing and $8.1 million will be allocated to the city's arts and culture fund.

The Lumberyard is a landmark, 100% affordable housing community coming to Aspen through a partnership between the City of Aspen and nationally recognized developer Gorman & Company. The project will deliver 277 high-quality rental homes in three thoughtfully designed four-story buildings ... income eligibility ranging from below 50% of Area Median Income (AMI) up to 240% AMI.

The project is located on an 11-acre site near the Airport Business Center along Highway 82, delivering 277 deed-restricted rental apartments throughout two phases. Gorman & Company was chosen as the Lumberyard development partner in July 2025. On March 24, 2026, Aspen City Council approved Resolution #042, Series of 2026 which approved a Deed Restriction, Ground Lease, Operating Agreement, Development Services Agreement and related documents for the purpose of completing financial closing "A" for the Lumberyard Affordable Housing Development.

Private developer Gorman & Company and their General Contractor Deneuve Construction have since begun construction of Phase 0 Infrastructure and Phase 1 Building #1 (104 units). Building #1 occupancy is planned for 2028. To deliver the full Lumberyard buildout of 277 rental units, the City plans to invest $250 million from its housing fund. The financial closing "B" is planned for May 2027 to begin construction of Lumberyard building #2 with 91 units and building #3 with 82 units.

Phase A includes the development of 104 units and significant improvements in public infrastructure... The work is anticipated to be completed in 2028. Phase B would then involve the development of the remaining 173 units and is expected to go before council for approval in May 2027, with full project completion projected for 2029.

1,390 Aspen voters, or 61.92% of the current count, supported Aspen Ballot Issue 2B, while 855 voters, or 38.08% of the count, opposed the question. ... The vote allows the city to enter into roughly $70 million in debt to help finance a 277-unit proposed affordable housing project.

The City of Aspen Ballot Issue 2B proposes to increase city debt by an amount not to exceed $70,000,000, with a total repayment cost not to exceed $152,550,000, for the purpose of financing the Lumberyard Affordable Housing Development. The ballot measure does not create any new taxes. The housing bonds will be paid for with revenues from the existing affordable housing real estate transfer tax.

rows.supporting_quote: In 2010 the Aspen City Council adopted the affordable housing credit program which provides a fourth option for meeting mitigation requirements associated with new development. ... The developer receives certificates for the voluntary deed-restricted housing. These certificates can then be sold on the free market to offset future housing requirements in other projects.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext. Original Burlingame Ranch paragraph was dropped because the cited source did not support all unit/phase/resident details.

rows.supporting_quote: Established in 2022, WMRHC is a Colorado 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to addressing the growing housing affordability crisis in the Roaring Fork and Colorado River valleys, from Aspen to Parachute. ... initiatives like our active Good Deeds program
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Original 2020/2023 incorporation timeline was corrected to match the source.

rows.supporting_quote: The West Mountain Regional Housing Coalition serves the Roaring Fork and Colorado River Valleys as a tax-exempt, Colorado 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation. ... Membership ... City of Aspen

Aspen City Council on Tuesday unanimously approved a $1 million contribution to the West Mountain Regional Housing Coalition’s Good Deeds program... This 2025 allocation represents Aspen's second major contribution to the Good Deeds program, as Aspen contributed $450,000 to the program in 2024.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Original largest-single-municipal-commitment wording was dropped.

rows.supporting_quote: Last year, Pitkin County contributed $1 million, the city of Aspen $450,000, the town of Snowmass Village $250,000, the city of Glenwood Springs $200,000, the town of Carbondale $100,000, for a total of $2 million in the program’s inaugural year.

Through the Good Deeds program, eligible homebuyers can receive financial assistance worth up to 30% of a home’s purchase price in exchange for a permanent deed restriction. These restrictions prohibit short-term rentals and second-home ownership, effectively converting free-market properties into stable, price-capped housing that supports the local workforce.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Replaced the older Aspen Journalism source because today's direct fetch did not support the specific operating terms asserted in the original paragraph.

rows.supporting_quote: Applicants must choose from three STR permit options... Lodging Exempt (STR-LE) Permit ... No annual limit on the number of nights per year the STR may operate. ... Owner-Occupied (STR-OO) Permit ... Limited to 120 STR rental nights per year. ... Classic (STR-C) Permit ... Limited by number in certain residential zone districts. ... There is no limit to the number of STR-C permits in these zone districts: Commercial (C-1), Commercial Core (CC), Lodge (L), Commercial Lodge (CL), Lodge Overlay (LP), Lodge Preservation Overlay (LO).
